title: Inspector UI Panel & Output Highlighting
goal: Build the Streamlit inspector panel UI including configuration controls, pass/fail badges, structured reason text, inspector model selector, and color-coded output highlighting (red=violation, green=validated, blue=informational).

in_scope: Render collapsible inspector panel (st.expander) gated by feature_inspector_enabled
Inspector configuration controls: validation format selector (JSON/Markdown/XML), required fields input, tone/persona expectation text inputs
Inspector model deployment selector (dropdown from discovered deployments)
Trigger inspector checks after inference results are available
Display pass/fail badges for each check per model response
Display structured reason text for semantic check failures
Implement output highlighting using Streamlit HTML: red=violation, green=validated, blue=informational
Store inspector configuration in session state for persistence across reruns
out_of_scope: Custom CSS theming beyond simple color-coded HTML

inputs: src/inspector.py — all check functions from Parts 02-04
Config feature flags for inspector controls
Discovered deployments for inspector model selector
PRD Week 2 section 4 — Output highlighting layer
outputs: src/app.py — updated with inspector panel UI and highlighting
checklist: Add inspector panel as st.expander('Output Inspector') gated by feature_inspector_enabled flag
Inside panel, render format selector: st.selectbox for JSON/Markdown/XML validation mode
Render st.text_input for required fields (comma-separated) when feature_inspector_required_fields is enabled
Render st.text_input for expected tone when feature_inspector_tone_check is enabled
Render st.text_input for expected persona when feature_inspector_persona_check is enabled
Render st.selectbox for inspector deployment selection (from discovered deployments) when semantic checks enabled
After inference results render, run enabled inspector checks against each model response
Display check results as pass/fail badges per model using st.success/st.error/st.info
For semantic check failures, display structured reason text from inspector model
Implement highlight rendering function using st.markdown with HTML: <span style='color:red'> for violations, green for validated, blue for informational
Gate highlight rendering behind feature_inspector_highlighting flag
Store inspector configuration (selected format, fields, tone, persona, deployment) in session state
acceptance_criteria: Inspector panel is visible only when feature_inspector_enabled is true
Format selector, required fields input, and tone/persona inputs render correctly
Inspector model deployment dropdown is populated from discovered deployments
Pass/fail badges render for each enabled check per model response
Semantic failure reasons display structured reason text
Violations render in red, validations in green, informational in blue
Highlighting does not alter original response text semantics
Inspector configuration persists in session state across reruns
Inspector panel is collapsible to avoid UI clutter
review_focus: Inspector panel does not interfere with existing results display layout
Color-coded highlighting uses safe HTML (no XSS vectors from model output)
Session state keys for inspector config don't collide with existing keys
Inspector checks run only when relevant flags are enabled (no unnecessary computation)
UI remains responsive during semantic inspector calls (consider spinner/progress)
risks_or_notes: Rendering raw model output in HTML highlight spans requires proper escaping to prevent XSS
Semantic checks may add noticeable latency — consider showing a spinner during inspector calls
Streamlit's st.markdown(unsafe_allow_html=True) is needed for color highlighting — use carefully
Required fields input parsing (comma-separated) should be robust to extra whitespace and empty entries
With many checks enabled across many models, the inspector section may become visually dense
definition_of_done: Inspector panel renders correctly with all configuration controls
All enabled checks display pass/fail badges per model response
Color-coded highlighting renders correctly with proper HTML escaping
Inspector panel is hidden when master toggle is disabled
Session state handles inspector configuration across reruns
UI remains clean and usable with 2-5 model responses
